UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a distinct financial profile managed by a user within the application. A single user account can have multiple profiles.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

userId.description: Reference to the external user account that owns this profile. (Relationship: User 1:N UserProfile)

name.description: A user-defined name for the financial profile (e.g., 'Personal Finances', 'Business Account A').

description.description: An optional detailed description for the financial profile.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user profile was updated.

Income.title: Income
Income.description: Records details of various income sources for a specific financial profile, used for profit/loss calculation and reporting.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Income entity.

profileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this income belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Income)

date.description: The date when the income was received.

source.description: The source of the income (e.g., 'Salary', 'Freelance Work', 'Investment Dividend').

client.description: The client or payer from whom the income was received, if applicable (e.g., for freelance income).

insuranceDetail.description: Specific details if this income is related to insurance (e.g., 'Insurance Claim Payout', 'Reimbursement').

debtDetail.description: Specific details if this income is related to debt (e.g., 'Personal Loan Repayment Received', 'Debt Settlement Income').

description.description: An optional detailed description for the income entry.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the income record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the income record was updated.

Expense.title: Expense
Expense.description: Logs details of expenditures for a specific financial profile, used for profit/loss calculation and reporting.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Expense entity.

profileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this expense belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Expense)

amount.description: The amount of the expense incurred.

date.description: The date when the expense was incurred.

itemService.description: A specific description of the item or service purchased (e.g., 'Groceries at Walmart', 'Monthly Bus Pass').

frequency.description: The frequency of the expense if it is recurring (e.g., 'one-time', 'daily', 'weekly', 'monthly', 'annually').

description.description: An optional detailed description for the expense entry.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the expense record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the expense record was updated.

SavingsAccount.title: SavingsAccount
SavingsAccount.description: Manages details of savings and investment accounts for a specific financial profile.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SavingsAccount entity.

profileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this savings account belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N SavingsAccount)

name.description: A descriptive name for the savings or investment account (e.g., 'Emergency Fund', 'Retirement Portfolio', 'College Savings').

type.description: The type of savings account (e.g., 'cash' for liquid funds, 'investment' for stocks/bonds, 'other').

currentAmount.description: The current amount held in the savings or investment account.

goalAmount.description: An optional target amount for this savings account to track progress towards a goal.

description.description: An optional detailed description for the savings account.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the savings account record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the savings account record was updated.

Debt.title: Debt
Debt.description: Tracks various debt obligations for a specific financial profile, including balances and payment details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Debt entity.

profileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this debt belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Debt)

name.description: A descriptive name for the debt (e.g., 'Visa Credit Card', 'Car Loan', 'House Mortgage').

type.description: The type of debt (e.g., 'credit card', 'house', 'car payment', 'mortgage', 'student loan', 'personal loan', 'other').

initialAmount.description: The original principal amount or initial balance of the debt.

currentBalance.description: The current outstanding balance of the debt.

interestRate.description: The annual interest rate of the debt (e.g., 0.05 for 5%).

minimumPayment.description: The minimum payment required for the debt.

dueDate.description: The next due date for a debt payment.

description.description: An optional detailed description for the debt entry.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the debt record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the debt record was updated.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records all financial transactions, providing a detailed log of money movement for a financial profile.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

profileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this transaction belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Transaction)

amount.description: The amount of money involved in the transaction.

date.description: The date when the transaction occurred.

type.description: The general type of the financial transaction (e.g., 'income', 'expense', 'transfer', 'debt_payment', 'savings_deposit', 'adjustment', 'other').

modeOfPayment.description: The method used for the payment (e.g., 'Credit Card', 'Bank Transfer', 'Cash', 'Debit Card', 'Cheque').

category.description: A broad category for the transaction (e.g., 'Groceries', 'Salary', 'Rent', 'Savings Transfer', 'Loan Repayment').

recipientSender.description: The recipient of the payment or the sender of the funds, depending on the transaction type.

description.description: An optional detailed description for the transaction.

incomeId.description: Optional reference to an Income entity if this transaction is linked to a specific income record. (Relationship: Income 1:N Transaction)

expenseId.description: Optional reference to an Expense entity if this transaction is linked to a specific expense record. (Relationship: Expense 1:N Transaction)

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the transaction record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the transaction record was updated.

definition.description: Stores user-specific financial profiles. Each profile is owned by a single user. The `userId` field explicitly confirms ownership and supports authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the profile.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific financial profile belonging to the user.

definition.description: Records income entries for a specific UserProfile. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'profileId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent profile.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent financial profile.

params.name: incomeId
params.description: The unique identifier for an income entry.

definition.description: Records expense entries for a specific UserProfile. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'profileId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent profile.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent financial profile.

params.name: expenseId
params.description: The unique identifier for an expense entry.

definition.description: Manages savings and investment accounts for a specific UserProfile. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'profileId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent profile.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent financial profile.

params.name: savingsAccountId
params.description: The unique identifier for a savings account.

definition.description: Tracks debt obligations for a specific UserProfile. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'profileId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent profile.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent financial profile.

params.name: debtId
params.description: The unique identifier for a debt entry.

definition.description: Records all financial transactions for a specific UserProfile. Includes denormalized 'userId' and 'profileId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent profile.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent financial profile.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a transaction entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for FinTrack Pro prioritizes **Authorization Independence** and **Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs)** by leveraging a strict hierarchical path structure combined with strategic denormalization. All user-specific data is nested under a top-level `/users/{userId}` collection. This immediately scopes all operations to the authenticated user, making security rules simple and robust.

**Authorization Independence:** This is achieved by mandating that every document in the hierarchy, starting from `UserProfile` down to `Income`, `Expense`, `SavingsAccount`, `Debt`, and `Transaction`, includes the `userId` field. While `UserProfile` naturally contains `userId`, for all subcollection documents (e.g., `incomes`, `expenses`), the `userId` (from the parent `/users/{userId}` path segment) and `profileId` (from the parent `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}` path segment) are explicitly denormalized into each document. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules to check parent ownership, which is critical for rule performance, atomic operations (transactions/batches), and debuggability. A rule for any document can simply verify `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` and `resource.data.profileId == userProfileId` without any cross-document reads.

**Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs):** The hierarchical structure directly supports efficient and secure `list` operations. Users can only perform queries within their own `/users/{request.auth.uid}/` subtree. For example, to list all incomes for a specific profile, the query would target `/users/{userId}/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/incomes`. The security rules will allow this `list` operation only if `userId` in the path matches `request.auth.uid`. The denormalized `userId` field within each `income` document provides an additional layer of verification, ensuring that even if a malicious actor somehow bypassed the path-based check (highly unlikely with correct rules), the document data itself would expose the mismatch, preventing unauthorized writes.

**Structural Segregation:** Each collection within the hierarchy (e.g., `userProfiles`, `incomes`, `expenses`) maintains a homogeneous security posture. All documents in a given collection within a user's path belong exclusively to that user and profile, simplifying security rules to basic ownership checks. There are no mixed-access data types (e.g., public/private) within the same collection.

**Access Modeling & Consistency:** The structure consistently applies path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/...`) for all private user data. Wildcards are semantically named (`userId`, `userProfileId`, `incomeId`, etc.) for clarity. This design ensures that the data model is predictable, easy to secure, and scales well for a multi-user financial tracking application.
